verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains all face the cytoplasm. It has no extracellular domain, no GPI anchor, no TM domain, and no outer-leaflet exposure. Checking all contextual buckets: (1) no documented cancer ecto-pool on the outer leaflet; (2) no documented cell-death-induced outer-leaflet surface display; (3) no germline/developmental-restricted outer-leaflet display with its own anchor; (4) no activation-induced translocation to the outer leaflet; (5) not a lysosomal TM protein; (6) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the extracellular face. Inner-leaflet lipid anchoring is explicitly excluded from surface accessibility. pMHC presentation of LYN peptides is the only potential 'surface' story, which maps to pmhc_only_intracellular. The protein body is strictly cytoplasmic-face and inaccessible to extracellular binders.
